Move immutable field resolution from Python to a generic Rust module
that reads existing K8s resources before rebuilding topology variables.
This prevents CAPI from overwriting immutable fields during updates.

- Add FieldMapping with jsonptr-based field extraction and label fallback
- Add kopium-generated OpenStackCluster CRD types
- Add compile-time git-describe via vergen-gix for cluster class naming
- Add compile-time assertion that VERGEN_GIT_DESCRIBE is non-empty
- Add PyO3 error boundary in driver.rs (create_exception + From<Error>)
- Add 15 unit tests for field resolution, 2 for cluster class naming
- Remove redundant Python integration tests covered by Rust tests
- Make rust_driver a required parameter in Cluster.__init__
- Update CI to use full git history for vergen-gix tag resolution

commit.message:
Universal agent primitives: search, read, write, link

Replaces the need for dozens of specialized tools with 4 composable
primitives that let the agent handle any lawyer workflow:

MatterStore (src/agent/matter-store.ts):
- Unified queryable store for all matter entities
- Matter-namespaced keys prevent cross-matter data access
- Full-text search across titles, content, and metadata
- Link validation ensures both endpoints exist in same matter
- Per-request isolation (no global singleton leaking across users)

Agent primitives:
- search_matter: full-text search across all entity types in a matter
- read_entity: get/list/get_links for any entity by type and ID
- write_entity: create or update any entity with type validation
- link_entities: typed bidirectional connections between entities

MCP server (src/mcp/litigation-server.ts):
- All 4 primitives registered as MCP tools alongside existing tools
- Any MCP client gets the same universal capabilities as the chat agent

System prompt updated to teach compose-from-primitives approach:
- Agent uses primitives for arbitrary workflows
- Specialized tools (conflicts, deadlines, Bates) kept for validated computation

Security fixes from GPT-5.4 review:
- Matter-namespaced entity keys in store
- Link validation (both endpoints must exist in same matter)
- Delete scoped to matter (won't remove other matters' links)
- Search metadata indexed for completeness

Agent module exports updated with new types and functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

all non-Esc keystrokes silently. Replace EscListenerGuard/spawn_esc_listener

characters typed during AI streaming and exposes them via stop_and_take().

streaming ends. Also fixes Ctrl+C during streaming (was silently
swallowed before; now correctly triggers abort).

fix(expenses): fix NOT NULL constraint crash when saving manual/reduction expenses

Root cause: explicit null values for NOT NULL DEFAULT columns bypass PostgreSQL DEFAULT:
  - extraction_warnings: NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]
  - requires_user_confirmation: NOT NULL DEFAULT false

These were set to: input.x ?? null — which inserts explicit NULL even when the
caller doesn't provide the field. PostgreSQL only uses DEFAULT when the column is
OMITTED from the INSERT, not when NULL is explicitly given.

Fix: use spread pattern to omit when value is absent, letting DB DEFAULT apply.
Same pattern already applied to cost_type in migration 122 fix.

Also:
- Add reductionError state + error display in reduction form (was silently failing)
- Clear reduction error on cancel

perf(kernels): dispatch per headdim via HEADDIM constexpr

Replace the runtime `headdim` parameter in both kernels with
`HEADDIM: tl.constexpr`.  Triton JIT compiles a separate PTX binary
for every distinct HEADDIM value — this is literal kernel dispatch per
head dimension.

Benefits per specialisation:
- Static loop unrolling: d-block loops change from `range(0, n_dblocks)`
  to `tl.static_range(HEADDIM // BLOCK_D)` with compile-time bounds,
  enabling full unrolling and optimal instruction scheduling.
- Dead-load elimination: the forward kernel previously loaded q_d1/q_d2/q_d3
  unconditionally before the K-block loop (all-zero masked for HEADDIM=64
  but the instructions were still issued); constexpr unrolling removes them.
- Mask elimination: `offs_d_cur < HEADDIM` is always True for aligned
  headdims (64/128/256 with BLOCK_D=64), so the predicate is dropped from
  every load/store in both kernels.
- Independent autotune caches: key now includes HEADDIM so each head-dim
  value gets its own independently tuned BLOCK_M/BLOCK_N/BLOCK_D config.

Forward kernel: restructured pre-loop Q loading from a manual 4-way
(q_d0/q_d1/q_d2/q_d3) pattern to a `q_blocks` tuple built with
`tl.static_range`; same for K loading + dot product accumulation inside
the K-block loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: cross-namespace KopiaRepository lookup in controllers

Both the kopiabackup and pvc-watcher controllers were only looking
for KopiaRepository in the backup/PVC's namespace. When the repo
lives in a different namespace (e.g. kopia-operator) while backups
are in app namespaces (e.g. hass), the repo was never found.

Now both controllers first check the local namespace, then fall back
to listing all KopiaRepositories cluster-wide. An error is returned
if no match or multiple matches are found (ambiguous).

* fix: auto-retarget when current target dies in combat (closes #321)

When fighting multiple creatures, defeating one left the player's
currentTarget pointing at the dead creature. The auto-attack logic
saw the dead target and defaulted to dodge instead of engaging the
next hostile.

Two-part fix:
1. Auto-attack logic (resolveTick): when currentTarget is invalid,
   call cycleTarget() to find the next valid hostile before falling
   back to dodge. Dodge only when no hostiles remain.
2. Defeat handling: after removing a defeated combatant, clear
   currentTarget for all remaining combatants that targeted them.

files and generates Ellmud-compatible SQL migrations matching the format
established in 003_seed_zones.sql.

- Skips cross-zone exits with clear warnings
- Maps CircleMUD exit flags to locked/hidden booleans

* fix(layout): preserve axis alignment in Midgaard zone layout

BFS correctly places east/west-connected rooms on the same row, but
force-directed relaxation and subsequent refinement phases broke this
alignment. The wall-road branch pulled inside-the-west-gate 4 cells
off the main-street row.

Two complementary fixes:

1. relaxationScore() — proportional diagonal penalty (DIAGONAL_PENALTY *
   max(offAxis, 1)) used only during relaxation phases. Makes large
   off-axis displacements progressively more expensive so the optimizer
   won't drag aligned rooms toward distant neighbours.

2. moveWouldBreakAlignment() — guard function that rejects moves breaking
   axis alignment between adjacent cardinal neighbours. Applied to
   relaxation, diagonal cascade, and direction-violation repair phases.
   For E/W exits protects Y alignment; for N/S exits protects X alignment.

asserting main-street corridor alignment.

* feat(layout): post-BFS cardinal alignment pass

Add Phase 5c to computeLayout — a cardinal alignment pass that runs
after direction violation repair and before occlusion fix.

Problem: BFS visit order is entry-point-sensitive. When the zone
designer uses a different entry room than the zone's actual entry
(e.g. due to room type mismatch between 'entrance' and 'entry'),
east/west-connected rooms can end up at different y levels, creating
visual right-angles where straight corridors should appear.

Solution: Build union-find groups for E/W exits (rooms that must
share the same y) and N/S exits (rooms that must share the same x).
For each group with misaligned coordinates, batch-shift all outlier
rooms plus their perpendicular subtrees to the majority coordinate.
Accept the shift only if the global layout score improves.

The cascade-shift approach preserves north/south topology: when an
E/W-misaligned room moves to match its row, its entire N/S subtree
moves with it, keeping wall-road correctly south of the gate.

Test: new entry-point independence test verifies E/W alignment holds
across 5 different BFS entry points on the same graph.

* fix(layout): general cardinal alignment without zone-specific logic

Replace the fragile cascade guard in Phase 5c cardinal alignment with
two general-purpose improvements:

1. Group-aware cascade: when the perpendicular subtree BFS encounters
   a room belonging to another multi-room alignment group, pull in the
   ENTIRE group so their internal alignment is preserved during shifts.

2. Chain-push collision resolution: instead of findNearestUnoccupied
   (which can displace rooms sideways, breaking alignments), push
   colliding rooms 1 step further in the shift direction (domino
   style), preserving relative ordering along the perpendicular axis.

3. Alignment-specific acceptance: use misaligned-pair count instead of
   general layoutScore, so alignment improvements aren't rejected by
   unrelated distance increases.

4. Largest-first processing: groups sorted by size so the most
   constrained corridors (e.g. 9-room main street) get priority.

All changes are topology-driven — no zone names, no hardcoded slugs.

* feat(layout): detect and resolve criss-crossing edges

Add Phase 5d to the BFS layout engine that detects and resolves
edge crossings — where a horizontal edge segment intersects a

or creating diagonal edges. Uses score-based acceptance (total

* feat(map): add Phase 5e selective row/column insertion for edge crossings

Add Phase 5e to computeLayout.ts as a fallback strategy after Phase 5d
(individual room moves). When edge crossings remain, Phase 5e tries
selective row/column insertion: for each H-V crossing at point (vX, hY),
compute 4 partition strategies (rooms above/below hY shift up/down,
rooms left/right of vX shift left/right). Try smallest partition first.
Apply shift temporarily, verify no diagonals/collisions, count crossings,
rollback if no improvement.

Key insight: when moving a GROUP of rooms, alignment checking must use
NEW neighbor positions (not old), so we apply moves first then check for
diagonals using updated positions.

* fix(layout): reduce crossings via row/column exchange in Phase 5e/9

Rewrite fixCrossingsByInsertion() to use full row/column exchange
instead of alignment-group shifts with chain-push cascades.

For each H×V crossing, swap ALL rooms on the H-edge's row with ALL
rooms on the V-edge's nearest endpoint row. This is collision-free
by construction and preserves E-W/N-S alignment within each row.

  by distance (prefer adjacent)
- Neighborhood-aware mismatch guard: counts mismatches for moved
  rooms AND their direct neighbors, catching bystander violations

  weighted (Phase 9, accept if crossingsFixed >= mismatchDelta)

* Replace row exchange with true grid expansion for crossing fixes

Phase 5e/9 now resolves edge crossings by inserting blank rows/columns
rather than swapping existing rows. For each H×V crossing:

1. Shift a partition of the grid to create a gap OUTSIDE the crossing
   edge's span (e.g. shift rooms above V's top by -1)
2. Move the crossing edge's alignment group into the gap

This genuinely adds space to the grid at crossing points. The partition
shift preserves all internal alignment; only the moved group's jump
can create direction mismatches (guarded by weighted acceptance).

Results: Midgaard crossings 9→3 (improved from 5 with exchange approach).

* fix(admin): correct player count in live rooms endpoint

Persistent zone rooms are registered as 'zone:<slug>' (e.g. 'zone:flooded-crypt')
but the admin API used exact equality checks (=== 'zone') to identify ZoneRoom
instances. This caused all persistent zone rooms to fall through to the generic
handler, returning no player/creature data.

Fix: Add isZoneRoomName() helper that matches both 'zone' and 'zone:<slug>'
patterns, and use it in all 4 affected endpoints:
- GET /admin/api/rooms/live (live room listing)
- GET /admin/api/rooms/:roomId (room detail)
- GET /admin/api/creatures (creature listing)

* fix: resolve admin spawn-creature crash from flat-to-nested template mismatch

The creature content store (PgCreatureDefinitionsStore) returns a flat
ContentEntity with top-level fields (maxHp, attack, minCount, etc.)
but CreatureManager.spawnSingleCreature() expects a nested CreatureTemplate
with stats: { maxHp, attack, ... } and spawnRules: { minCount, ... }.

The unsafe 'as unknown as CreatureTemplate' cast masked this shape mismatch,
causing template.stats to be undefined and crashing on template.stats.maxHp.

- Add contentEntityToCreatureTemplate() helper that maps flat → nested shape
- Replace both unsafe casts in the spawn-creature and spawn-creature (zone) paths
- Update test fixture to use flat ContentEntity shape matching real store output

  a new roomGraphRooms[] field on every zone detail response.
- Client: roomOccupancy useMemo falls back to roomGraphRooms when zone
  data is unavailable, and a new displayRooms memo normalises both
  sources for the Room Graph tab.
- Types: AdminZoneDetail gains roomGraphRooms; LiveRoomDetail gains
  the matching client-side field.

  coverage, and creature→room ID alignment.

* fix: spawned creatures now appear in admin room list for static zones

The roomOccupancy memo in LiveRoomDetail prioritized zoneData.rooms
(from a separate /zones/:slug API call fetched once at mount) over
roomGraphRooms (from the same /rooms/:roomId response as creatures).
When these two data sources diverged, creatures were silently dropped
because their currentRoomId didn't match any key in the occupancy map.

Fix:
- Swap priority to prefer roomGraphRooms (same response as creatures)
  over zoneData.rooms (separate, potentially stale fetch)
- Add defensive auto-creation of map entries for creatures whose
  currentRoomId doesn't match any known room, preventing silent drops

Tests:
- 2 server integration tests for static zone spawn→display flow
- 8 client unit tests for roomOccupancy logic covering the fix,
  the old buggy behavior, and defensive fallback scenarios
